ai_initiatives.text: DCAA-compliant automated time allocation engine — uses rule-based logic to distribute labor hours across contracts, reducing audit exceptions by automating complex A&D timekeeping rules

leadership.name: Chris Thomas
leadership.role: CEO — ex-UKG A&D division, drove 119% bookings growth

recent_news.title: Solen Software Group acquires AutoTime
recent_news.summary: AutoTime joins Solen portfolio, continuing 20+ year focus on DCAA-compliant time and labor tracking for A&D contractors.

key_evidence.title: DCAA Compliance Leadership
key_evidence.text: AutoTime is the leading time and labor tracking solution purpose-built for A&D. Customers include top defense contractors using it for DCAA audit compliance, complex labor tracking, and payroll accuracy. Modules include TTA (Time and Task Allocation) for automated labor distribution across contracts.

key_evidence.title: CEO Track Record
key_evidence.text: CEO Chris Thomas previously managed an A&D acquisition at UKG driving 119% bookings growth and nearly doubling EBITDA in the first year, overseeing $11M annual revenue. Deep domain expertise in defense contractor workforce management.

key_evidence.title: Defense Contractor Customer Base
key_evidence.text: AutoTime's customer roster includes the top 5 U.S. defense prime contractors. The platform handles complex labor distribution requirements unique to government contracting — DCAA audit trail, contract-level time allocation, and multi-rate payroll calculations.

key_evidence.title: 20+ Year Market Tenure
key_evidence.text: Founded in 2003, AutoTime has served the A&D industry for over 20 years. The long tenure demonstrates deep domain lock-in and high switching costs — critical for PE investment thesis around sticky, mission-critical vertical SaaS.
Solen actively hiring Chief AI Officer to identify, scale, and operationalize AI use cases across all portfolio companies — developing shared AI patterns, frameworks, and governance standards
Solen building AI Center of Excellence (COE): hiring COE AI Software Engineer and COE Technical Lead in São Paulo, Brazil — shared AI engineering team to serve all portfolio companies

AutoTime.narrative_summary: AutoTime is a 20+ year market leader in DCAA-compliant time and labor tracking for the Aerospace & Defense industry. The platform serves elite defense primes including Lockheed Martin, General Dynamics, Collins Aerospace, Rolls-Royce, and L3Harris — with complex labor distribution across government contracts, audit-ready timekeeping, and multi-rate payroll. CEO Chris Thomas brings proven PE execution, having driven 119% bookings growth at UKG's A&D division. AutoTime was acquired by Solen in February 2025. The company is actively hiring software engineering leadership, signaling a product modernization push. Core tech includes SAP integration, cloud-hosted SaaS, and REST APIs.

US citrus growers representing 64% of orange production

hiring_signals: ERP Developer ($68K-$116K, Spokane Valley, WA — Glassdoor listing)

key_evidence.text: Spokane Software Systems actively hiring full-time ERP Developer ($68K-$116K) in Spokane Valley, WA — signals investment in platform modernization despite small 9-person team size

key_evidence.title: Agricultural ERP Market Dominance
key_evidence.text: Spokane Software Systems produces the leading agricultural ERP system for growers, packers, and shippers throughout the U.S. for over 37 years. 64% of US orange varieties and 80% of US lemon varieties flow through the Spokane System — extraordinary market penetration for a 9-person company.

key_evidence.title: PTI/GTIN Compliance Module
key_evidence.text: PackPoint module addresses Produce Traceability Initiative (PTI) and Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) requirements. Critical for produce industry regulatory compliance and food safety traceability mandates.

key_evidence.title: Sun Pacific Customer Reference
key_evidence.text: Sun Pacific, one of the largest citrus growers and shippers in the United States, uses the Spokane System for its packing operations and grower accounting.

key_evidence.title: Full-Suite Agricultural Operations
key_evidence.text: Modules include packing house operations, shipping and logistics, grower accounting, field labor tracking, grove management, and regulatory compliance. Deep vertical integration covering the entire citrus supply chain from grove to retail.

key_evidence.title: IBM Power Systems Architecture
key_evidence.text: Platform-independent clients built on IBM Power Systems technology for reliability, performance, and ease of use. Core ERP runs on AS/400 with RPG programming and DB2 database — legacy architecture but proven for 37+ years of continuous agricultural operations.
Solen actively hiring Chief AI Officer to identify, scale, and operationalize AI use cases across all portfolio companies — developing shared AI patterns, frameworks, and governance standards
Solen building AI Center of Excellence (COE): hiring COE AI Software Engineer and COE Technical Lead in São Paulo, Brazil — shared AI engineering team to serve all portfolio companies

Spokane.narrative_summary: Spokane Software Systems is the dominant ERP platform for the U.S. citrus industry with extraordinary market penetration — 64% of US orange varieties and 80% of US lemon varieties flow through the Spokane System. Founded 37+ years ago, the platform runs on IBM Power Systems (AS/400) with RPG and DB2 — legacy technology but proven. Key customer Sun Pacific ($100M revenue, 480 employees) is one of the largest citrus growers in the US. PackPoint module addresses PTI and GTIN compliance for produce traceability. Full-suite includes packing house operations, shipping/logistics, grower accounting, and payroll. The extreme market dominance (product_differentiation 4.20) represents the strongest competitive moat in the Solen portfolio, but the AS/400 architecture means significant modernization investment would be needed before AI features are feasible.

This is the least-verifiable company in the portfolio and scores should be treated with appropriate uncertainty.

recent_news.title: Primate joins Solen Software Group

key_evidence.title: Public Health EHR Market Leader
key_evidence.text: Champ Software develops Nightingale Notes, an EHR system built specifically for public health departments. More than 145 community health agencies trust Nightingale Notes to track, report, and share vital information. Uses the Omaha System standardized taxonomy for evidence-based outcomes documentation.

key_evidence.title: Cloud-Native HIPAA Architecture
key_evidence.text: Nightingale Notes is a cloud-based SaaS solution hosted on Amazon Web Services. No data is stored on local machines; fully HIPAA compliant. Features include batch billing, scheduling, and 100% time tracking for public health workers.

key_evidence.title: SES Partnership for Health Information Exchange
key_evidence.text: Partnership with Secure Exchange Solutions adds Direct messaging to Nightingale Notes, enabling public health agencies to securely exchange patient data with hospitals, labs, and other care providers.

key_evidence.title: 40-Year Heritage
key_evidence.text: Founded in 1985 in Mankato, Minnesota by David Rosebaugh. The 40-year tenure in public health software demonstrates deep domain expertise and high customer loyalty — critical attributes for PE investment in vertical SaaS.

Champ.narrative_summary: Champ Software develops Nightingale Notes, the leading EHR system purpose-built for public health departments, serving 145+ community health agencies nationwide. Cloud-hosted on AWS with full HIPAA compliance, the platform uses the Omaha System standardized taxonomy for evidence-based outcomes documentation. Features include batch billing, scheduling, 100% time tracking, and secure health information exchange via the SES Direct messaging partnership. Founded in 1985 by David Rosebaugh in Mankato, Minnesota; CEO Scott Dunnewind acquired ownership in 2021. The 40-year domain tenure, regulatory moat (HIPAA/Omaha System), and 145+ agency install base create significant switching costs.

ai_initiatives.text: GridGuardian anomaly detection — real-time SCADA/EMS monitoring with automated alerting for grid anomalies and operational deviations

recent_news.title: Primate joins Solen Software Group
recent_news.summary: Primate Technologies, founded in 2001 in Melbourne, Florida, joined the Solen Software Group in 2024 to accelerate growth in mission-critical control room software.

key_evidence.title: GridGuardian Data Integration Platform
key_evidence.text: GridGuardian is an intelligent data processing engine that ingests, aggregates, and processes real-time and historical control room data. Consolidates SCADA, EMS, OMS, and other operational platforms through SQL, MQTT, XML, and custom APIs into unified operational visibility.

key_evidence.title: Automated Anomaly Detection
key_evidence.text: Automated anomaly detection isolates abnormal patterns in real time for predictive maintenance. Uses advanced algorithms to turn complex, high-volume data into precise operational intelligence. Smart alerts prioritize critical updates via rule-based notification system.

key_evidence.title: NERC Compliance & Event Tracking
key_evidence.text: Tracks reportable operational events in real time including line outages and restoration intervals. Auto-timestamps each event providing accurate, auditable records for NERC reporting. Replaces manual note-taking with automated compliance documentation.

key_evidence.title: Business Impact Metrics
key_evidence.text: Claims $150K+ annual labor savings (96% faster grid updates), prevents $6.7B blackout-scale losses, stops $1M/day NERC violation penalties, and reduces average outage recovery from 5.8 hours to minutes.

key_evidence.title: Product Suite
key_evidence.text: Full suite: GridGuardian (data integration + anomaly detection), BlackBoard (crystal-clear multi-display visualization), TileViewer (secure multi-platform control room access), TileBuilder (custom display layout designer). NERC CIP architecture throughout.

key_evidence.title: Solen Portfolio Company
key_evidence.text: Founded in 2001 in Melbourne, Florida. Joined Solen Software Group in 2024. Exemplifies Solen thesis: mission-critical, hyper-niche vertical SaaS with deep domain expertise and high switching costs.
Solen actively hiring Chief AI Officer to identify, scale, and operationalize AI use cases across all portfolio companies — developing shared AI patterns, frameworks, and governance standards
Solen building AI Center of Excellence (COE): hiring COE AI Software Engineer and COE Technical Lead in São Paulo, Brazil — shared AI engineering team to serve all portfolio companies

Primate.narrative_summary: Primate Technologies builds GridGuardian, an intelligent data processing engine for mission-critical utility control rooms. The platform ingests real-time and historical data from SCADA, EMS, OMS, and other operational systems via SQL, MQTT, XML, and custom APIs, consolidating fragmented data streams into unified operational visibility. Key capabilities include automated anomaly detection that isolates abnormal patterns in real time, smart alerting, and automated NERC compliance event tracking. The company claims $150K+ annual labor savings per deployment and 96% faster grid updates. Product suite includes BlackBoard (multi-display visualization), TileViewer (secure remote access), and TileBuilder (custom layouts). Founded 2001 in Melbourne, FL, joined Solen in 2024.

AI-powered video telematics with dash cameras detecting risky behaviors: phone use, fatigue, tailgating, smoking, seatbelt violations — real-time driver coaching

Track Star.narrative_summary: Track Star International provides a unified fleet management and enterprise asset management platform processing millions of GPS points daily. The Version 6 browser-based platform combines GPS tracking, telematics, EAM, predictive maintenance, AI-powered video telematics, and compliance automation. Founded in 2000 by ex-USAF navigation expert Michael Hughes; now led by CEO Gabriel Reza. The company acquired ThingTech to expand IoT capabilities and rebuilt its AVLS product on Esri ArcGIS. Serves government fleets, law enforcement, utilities, equipment rental, and education. Supports 10+ hardware partners (Sierra Wireless, CalAmp, SmartWitness). Revenue reported at $24.4M. Part of Solen since 2021.

SMRTR.narrative_summary: SMRTR was formed in July 2025 from the merger of Dash (AP automation) and S4i Systems (compliance/content management), creating a unified automation platform for manufacturing and food & beverage distribution. CEO Susanne Moore leads the combined entity from Cottonwood Heights, Utah. The platform spans AP Automation, Food Safety Compliance, Electronic Proof of Delivery, Supplier Onboarding, Nutrition Labeling, and Document Management — integrating with 6 major ERP systems (SYSPRO, SAP, JD Edwards, Infor, VIP Beverage, NCR). AI/ML capabilities include predictive maintenance, route optimization, and compliance anomaly detection. Strategic partnerships with VAI (S2K Food ERP) and industry distributors.

recent_news.title: FMSI Relaunches as Independent Company
recent_news.summary: FMSI relaunched brand after December 2025 divestiture from UKG, with renewed focus on branch performance for banks and credit unions. Multiple new clients signed within 60 days.

key_evidence.text: FMSI OneCX combines appointment scheduling, lobby management, staffing analytics, and staff scheduling into one integrated solution for banks and credit unions. Over 25 years of workforce optimization expertise serving 140+ financial institutions.

key_evidence.title: Apple Federal Credit Union Win
key_evidence.text: Apple Federal Credit Union, a $2B Virginia-based credit union with 25 locations, adopted FMSI's Omnix Staff Scheduler, Omnix Interactive Dashboard, and Omnix Performance Analytics for branch-wide workforce optimization.

key_evidence.title: 40% Lobby Wait Time Reduction
key_evidence.text: Regional credit union achieved 40% reduction in lobby wait times through FMSI's structured appointment tools without additional hiring — demonstrating measurable ROI for branch operations.

key_evidence.title: Post-UKG Independence
key_evidence.text: FMSI was acquired by Kronos in 2017, became part of UKG in 2020, and was divested as independent company in December 2025. The relaunch positions FMSI as 'the branch advisory hub' — shifting from transactional to advisory banking.

key_evidence.title: Appli AI Partnership
key_evidence.text: Partnership with Appli adds AI-powered financial calculators helping members make informed decisions before branch visits. Combined with FMSI scheduling and analytics, creates a connected member journey from digital engagement to in-branch advisory.

FMSI.narrative_summary: FMSI provides the OneCX platform combining appointment scheduling, lobby management, staffing analytics, and workforce optimization for 140+ banks and credit unions. Founded in 1990 by W. Michael Scott, acquired by Kronos/UKG in 2017, and relaunched as an independent company in March 2026 after UKG divestiture. Major customers include Apple Federal Credit Union ($2B, 25 locations) and Fort Knox FCU. A regional credit union achieved 40% lobby wait time reduction using FMSI tools. Recent Appli partnership adds AI-powered financial calculators. Generating $5.3M annual revenue with rapid post-relaunch customer acquisition (new clients signed within 60 days).

ai_initiatives.text: Automated route optimization algorithms for waste hauling — optimizes driver routes across container pickups and deliveries

leadership.name: James Moser
leadership.role: Founder & President — MS in IT, 15+ years mission-critical app development

recent_news.title: Solen Software Group Acquires Cairn Applications
recent_news.summary: Solen acquired Cairn Applications in December 2025. Cairn supports 350+ customers managing 65,000+ dumpsters and ~$150M in annual commerce.

key_evidence.title: Box Tracker Flagship Platform
key_evidence.text: Flagship product Box Tracker manages order management, logistics, billing, merchant services, asset tracking, employee/driver tracking, GPS navigation, CRM, and automated customer communication for independent dumpster companies. Top features: real-time tracking, route optimization, inventory management, automated reporting, customer portal.

key_evidence.title: Market Scale & Commerce
key_evidence.text: Supports 350+ customers managing 65,000+ dumpsters and containers, facilitating approximately $150 million in annual commerce across the United States and Canada.

key_evidence.title: Multi-Product Suite
key_evidence.text: Three products: Box Tracker (dumpster/roll-off container management), Scale House (landfill/transfer station operations), and Route Tracker (commercial trash, curbside pickup, port-a-potty management). Covers the full waste hauling operations workflow.

key_evidence.title: Born in the Cloud Architecture
key_evidence.text: Platform is 'born in the cloud' — requires only internet and a browser. Device, platform, and geographically agnostic. MapIt! feature provides deep Google Maps integration for real-time visibility of drivers, work orders, containers, and local traffic.

key_evidence.title: Solen Acquisition Context
key_evidence.text: Founded in 2008 by James Moser (MS in IT, 15+ years experience) in Nashua, NH. Cairn continues operating with autonomy within Solen, with access to shared playbooks in growth strategy, digital transformation, and operational excellence.
Solen actively hiring Chief AI Officer to identify, scale, and operationalize AI use cases across all portfolio companies — developing shared AI patterns, frameworks, and governance standards
Solen building AI Center of Excellence (COE): hiring COE AI Software Engineer and COE Technical Lead in São Paulo, Brazil — shared AI engineering team to serve all portfolio companies

Cairn Applications.narrative_summary: Cairn Applications is the leading cloud SaaS platform for independent waste hauling operations, managing 350+ customers, 65,000+ dumpsters, and ~$150M in annual commerce across the US and Canada. The product suite includes Box Tracker (dumpster management with real-time GPS tracking, route optimization, billing, and CRM), Scale House (landfill operations), and Route Tracker (commercial trash/curbside). Founded in 2008 by James Moser in Nashua, NH. The 'born in the cloud' platform is device-agnostic with deep Google Maps integration (MapIt!). Acquired by Solen in December 2025. The waste hauling vertical presents AI opportunities in route optimization, predictive demand forecasting, and automated dispatch.

ViaPeople.narrative_summary: ViaPeople provides flexible, I/O psychology-founded talent management software with AI Instant Insights, 360-degree feedback (with bias-reduction), deal team feedback, succession planning (9-box), automated promotions, and compensation planning (via SpiraLinks merger). Founded in 2002 by PhD organizational psychologists and headquartered in Plainsboro, NJ. CEO Jerry Dunne leads the ~31-person team. SOC 2 certified. Serves PE firms, investment banks, and large enterprises with complex talent structures. Case studies include a large retailer increasing leadership diversity and a pharma company building internal talent pipelines. Premium pricing ($5,000+/user). Significant AI R&D investment underway.

key_evidence.title: Series A & Investor Backing
key_evidence.text: Raised $4M Series A in January 2019 led by BIP Capital (now Panoramic Ventures). Total funding: $5.45M. Based in Atlanta, Georgia. Founded in 2014.
Solen actively hiring Chief AI Officer to identify, scale, and operationalize AI use cases across all portfolio companies — developing shared AI patterns, frameworks, and governance standards
Solen building AI Center of Excellence (COE): hiring COE AI Software Engineer and COE Technical Lead in São Paulo, Brazil — shared AI engineering team to serve all portfolio companies
ThingTech officially integrated into Track Star platform — combined fleet management and IoT asset tracking under unified brand

ThingTech.narrative_summary: ThingTech provides an IoT-based enterprise asset management platform with three SaaS products: manageIT (EAM), tracIT (vehicle tracking/telematics), and routeIT (schedule/route optimization). Built on CalAmp Connect IoT with out-of-the-box connected sensors for predictive maintenance and real-time condition monitoring. Government customers include NAIPTA, Fulton County GA, State of Delaware, and Berkshire Regional Transit. Also on Salesforce AppExchange. Founded in 2014 in Atlanta by Tim Quinn and Brian Corcoran. Raised $5.45M (Series A led by BIP Capital/Panoramic Ventures). Acquired by Track Star International in 2024, creating a combined fleet + IoT asset platform under Solen.

ai_initiatives.text: Compliance automation engine — automates PTASP (Public Transportation Agency Safety Plan) data collection and reporting for FTA compliance

leadership.name: Mark Anderson
leadership.role: Founder & President — continues post-Solen acquisition

recent_news.title: Solen Software Group Acquires Trackit LLC
recent_news.summary: Solen acquired Trackit LLC in February 2026. Mark Anderson continues as President. More than 100 transit agencies rely on Trackit for PTASP compliance.

key_evidence.title: Transit-Specific SaaS Platform
key_evidence.text: TrackIt provides a configurable, paperless system for public transit agencies — replacing paper-based workflows with searchable digital records. Modules include attendance, claims tracking, compliance/doc management, customer service, eLearning, event logging, safety reporting, form management, and HR.

key_evidence.title: 100+ Transit Agency Adoption
key_evidence.text: More than 100 transit agencies have adopted Trackit to develop agency-wide safety plans with sophisticated record keeping. Data entered once is shared across departments with permissions-based access, breaking down information silos.

key_evidence.title: PTASP Compliance Leadership
key_evidence.text: TrackIt enables transit agencies to comply with PTASP (Public Transportation Agency Safety Plan) requirements under FTA regulations. The platform facilitates communication and workflow between departments while providing audit-ready compliance documentation.

key_evidence.title: Rapid Deployment
key_evidence.text: Cloud-hosted platform enables rapid deployment in under 1 day. Trackit Assessment mobile app available on Google Play for field operations and safety inspections.
Solen actively hiring Chief AI Officer to identify, scale, and operationalize AI use cases across all portfolio companies — developing shared AI patterns, frameworks, and governance standards
Solen building AI Center of Excellence (COE): hiring COE AI Software Engineer and COE Technical Lead in São Paulo, Brazil — shared AI engineering team to serve all portfolio companies

TrackIt Transit.narrative_summary: TrackIt Transit is a transit-specific cloud SaaS platform deployed by 100+ public transit agencies for PTASP compliance, safety management, and paperless operations. Modules span attendance, claims tracking, compliance documentation, customer service, eLearning, event logging, safety reporting, and HR management. Data entered once flows across departments with permissions-based access. Founded in 2013 by Mark Anderson, who continues as President post-Solen acquisition (February 2026). SWTA member. Mobile app (Trackit Assessment) available on Google Play. Rapid deployment capability (<1 day). The transit safety compliance niche presents AI opportunities in predictive safety analytics and automated incident reporting.

Automated caption agents replacing traditional human steno captioning — faster, more accurate, and privacy-preserving

recent_news.title: FCC Grants IP CTS Certification
recent_news.summary: FCC granted NexTalk Software Inc. conditional IP CTS certification (24-month term through June 2027), enabling SpeechPath captioned telephone service.

recent_news.title: Solen Ventures Acquires NexTalk
recent_news.summary: Solen Ventures LLC acquired NexTalk with Foley & Lardner as legal advisor. Scott Cummings continues as CEO.

recent_news.title: SpeechPath FCC Approval
recent_news.summary: NexTalk approved by FCC as IP CTS provider with SpeechPath — automated speech-to-text captioning achieving industry-leading accuracy and speed.

recent_news.title: Carahsoft Government Partnership
recent_news.summary: NexTalk accessibility solutions available through Carahsoft for federal and state government procurement.

key_evidence.title: FCC-Approved IP CTS Provider
key_evidence.text: FCC granted NexTalk conditional IP CTS certification in June 2025. SpeechPath uses a Text Engine Server to convert speech to text — faster and more accurate than human steno captioning, and fully private/confidential. Funded by FCC TRS Fund.

key_evidence.title: Healthfirst $1M Impact
key_evidence.text: Healthfirst (Medicare/Medicaid health plan) achieved nearly $1 million increase in government rebates through improved 5-Star CMS Program ratings after deploying NexTalk's accessibility solutions for deaf, hard of hearing, and non-English speaking customers.

key_evidence.title: 50 Million Market Opportunity
key_evidence.text: 50 million deaf and hard-of-hearing people across the U.S. and Canada represent the addressable market. Products: Access Contact Center (agent-side TTY/RTT + interpreter), SpeechPath (AI speech-to-text), Access Home (consumer IP CTS), SimpliciTTY (legacy TTY).

key_evidence.title: Enterprise Sales Leadership
key_evidence.text: CEO Scott Cummings brings enterprise SaaS sales expertise from SAP (VP West Region for SuccessFactors and High Growth Market), Fujitsu America, and RightNow Technologies. EVP Sales Gary Tanner driving expansion into government and healthcare verticals.

key_evidence.title: Carahsoft Government Channel
key_evidence.text: NexTalk's Access Government package available through Carahsoft, the trusted government IT solutions provider. Enables federal and state agency procurement of ADA-compliant communication tools.
Solen actively hiring Chief AI Officer to identify, scale, and operationalize AI use cases across all portfolio companies — developing shared AI patterns, frameworks, and governance standards
Solen building AI Center of Excellence (COE): hiring COE AI Software Engineer and COE Technical Lead in São Paulo, Brazil — shared AI engineering team to serve all portfolio companies

NexTalk.narrative_summary: NexTalk provides FCC-approved accessible communication software serving 200+ companies and 50 million deaf/hard-of-hearing individuals across the US and Canada. The SpeechPath platform uses AI-powered automated speech recognition achieving industry-leading Word Error Rate and latency — replacing traditional human steno captioning with faster, more accurate, and privacy-preserving technology. Key products: Access Contact Center, SpeechPath (AI speech-to-text), Access Home (consumer IP CTS), and Access Government. Major customer Healthfirst achieved nearly $1M in government rebate increases. CEO Scott Cummings (ex-SAP VP) and EVP Sales Gary Tanner lead growth into government (via Carahsoft) and healthcare. Acquired by Solen in May 2025; FCC IP CTS certification granted June 2025.

executives.name: Susanne Moore
executives.role: CEO, S4i Systems dba SMRTR (oversees Dash post-merger)

ai_initiatives.text: AP Robot claims 'Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology to automate AP invoice processing' — in practice this is OCR-based data extraction with rules-based 3-way matching, achieving 90%+ accuracy on standard invoices
Duplicate and fraudulent invoice detection — rule-based pattern matching, not ML

leadership.name: Jim Van Hecke
leadership.role: President — co-designer of DDX, document management specialist (no AI background)

leadership.name: Steve Bayliss
leadership.role: VP R&D — traditional software design and application development

Manufacturing and distribution enterprises using SYSPRO, Aptean, Syteline

recent_news.title: Dash joins Solen Software Group
recent_news.summary: Dash Development Group acquired by Solen Software Group as part of their vertical SaaS portfolio strategy.

recent_news.title: Dash + S4i Systems merge to form SMRTR
recent_news.summary: Dash merged with S4i Systems to create SMRTR, combining AP automation with compliance solutions for food & beverage and manufacturing industries.

key_evidence.title: DDX: Only Doc Mgmt Pre-Integrated with SYSPRO
key_evidence.text: DASH DDX is the only document management software offered pre-integrated with SYSPRO ERP, in individual modules or pre-bundled suites. Founded in 1998, over 25 years of manufacturing-focused document automation.

key_evidence.title: AP Robot Invoice Automation
key_evidence.text: AP Robot uses OCR-based invoice capture with automated 3-way matching (invoice vs PO vs receipt). Claims 90%+ accuracy, processing invoices in under 60 seconds. Marketing describes this as 'AI technology' but technical details indicate rules-based matching with OCR.

key_evidence.title: Cloud/On-Premise/Hybrid Deployment
key_evidence.text: Dash offers three deployment models: fully managed cloud, on-premise (customizable), and hybrid. This legacy flexibility is typical of 25-year-old enterprise software products, not cloud-native architecture.

key_evidence.title: Entity Clarification
key_evidence.text: IMPORTANT: This is Dash Development Group (dashdev.com), NOT Dash ComplyOps (dashsdk.com). Previous scoring rounds incorrectly conflated these two companies. Dash Development Group is a traditional document management/AP automation company for manufacturing — not a cloud security/compliance platform.
Solen actively hiring Chief AI Officer to identify, scale, and operationalize AI use cases across all portfolio companies — developing shared AI patterns, frameworks, and governance standards
Solen building AI Center of Excellence (COE): hiring COE AI Software Engineer and COE Technical Lead in São Paulo, Brazil — shared AI engineering team to serve all portfolio companies

Dash.narrative_summary: Dash Development Group (dashdev.com) is a 25-year-old document management and AP automation company serving manufacturing and distribution enterprises. Core products are DDX (document management pre-integrated with SYSPRO ERP) and AP Robot (OCR-based invoice processing with rules-based 3-way matching). The company offers cloud, on-premise, and hybrid deployment. Leadership team (Jim Van Hecke, Steve Bayliss) has deep document management expertise but no AI/ML background. AP Robot's 'AI' marketing describes OCR + rules-based automation, not machine learning. Joined Solen Software Group in 2024 and merged with S4i Systems to form SMRTR in July 2025. NOTE: Previous scoring rounds incorrectly used data from Dash ComplyOps (dashsdk.com), a completely different company. All scores corrected in v4.3 audit.
